statements.impact_statement: CVE-2026-34040 is a Docker Engine AuthZ-plugin bypass in the Moby server: a server-side request handler accepts an oversized request body that the AuthZ plugin then fails to evaluate. The quay.io/stackstate/opentelemetry-target-allocator image ships only the targetallocator Go binary (images/opentelemetry-target-allocator/Dockerfile in StackVista/docker-images), built from ./cmd/otel-allocator in open-telemetry/opentelemetry-operator. github.com/docker/docker enters the binary transitively through github.com/prometheus/prometheus/discovery; the target allocator and the operator only link the client-side packages of github.com/docker/docker and do not contain the Moby server (cmd/dockerd, daemon, api/server), so the vulnerable AuthZ-plugin request path is not present in the execute path. This is confirmed upstream in open-telemetry/opentelemetry-operator#4926: "github.com/docker/docker is a transitive dependency pulled in by the github.com/prometheus/prometheus/discovery package. It only uses the client side of the docker package, whereas the vulnerabilities affect the server side." The fix lives at the new github.com/moby/moby/v2 module path (Docker Engine 29.x / v2.0.0-beta.14); Prometheus has migrated in prometheus/prometheus#18433, and once the next prometheus-operator release picks up that Prometheus version and the OpenTelemetry Operator bumps to it, the docker/docker dependency will disappear from the target allocator entirely and this VEX will become moot.

CVE-2026-41567 is a PATH-resolution flaw in the Moby server's docker cp implementation: when the daemon decompresses an archive into a target container it resolves xz/unpigz/gzip helper binaries via the container filesystem's PATH while running as host root, letting a malicious image trojanise the helper. The vulnerable code lives in the Docker Engine server (daemon/archive.go and friends). The quay.io/stackstate/opentelemetry-target-allocator image ships only the targetallocator Go binary; it contains no dockerd, no archive-extraction code path, and does not invoke docker cp on behalf of the user. github.com/docker/docker is pulled in transitively through github.com/prometheus/prometheus/discovery, and per upstream open-telemetry/opentelemetry-operator#4926 only the client-side packages are used: "It only uses the client side of the docker package, whereas the vulnerabilities affect the server side." The fix lives at the new github.com/moby/moby/v2 module path (Docker Engine 29.x / v2.0.0-beta.14); Prometheus has migrated in prometheus/prometheus#18433, and once the next prometheus-operator release picks up that Prometheus version and the OpenTelemetry Operator bumps to it, the docker/docker dependency will disappear from the target allocator entirely and this VEX will become moot.

CVE-2026-42306 is a TOCTOU race in the Moby server's docker cp bind-mount handling: a container process can redirect a bind mount to an arbitrary host path between the daemon's path resolution and its mount syscall. The vulnerable code lives in the Docker Engine server (daemon/cluster/executor and the cp/mount path). The quay.io/stackstate/opentelemetry-target-allocator image ships only the targetallocator Go binary; it contains no dockerd, performs no bind-mount setup, and is not invoked as a Docker daemon. github.com/docker/docker is pulled in transitively through github.com/prometheus/prometheus/discovery, and per upstream open-telemetry/opentelemetry-operator#4926 only the client-side packages are used: "It only uses the client side of the docker package, whereas the vulnerabilities affect the server side." The fix lives at the new github.com/moby/moby/v2 module path (Docker Engine 29.x / v2.0.0-beta.14); Prometheus has migrated in prometheus/prometheus#18433, and once the next prometheus-operator release picks up that Prometheus version and the OpenTelemetry Operator bumps to it, the docker/docker dependency will disappear from the target allocator entirely and this VEX will become moot.
